The Sages taught: you shall afflict your souls” might sit in the sun or in the cold to suffer the the verse states: “And you shall not do any labor, just as labor is sit and do nothing, so too, affliction soul is also sit and do nothing.

And say that when sits in the sun and it is hot for him, we do not say to him: Get up and sit in the shade. sits in the shade and it is cold for him, we do not say to him: Get up and sit in the sun. similar labor. Just as labor you did not distinguish so too, you do not distinguish affliction,

It was taught another “You shall afflict your souls” might one sit in the sun or the cold and be uncomfortable; the the verse states: “And you shall not do any labor” Just as labor is something incurs in other circumstances, so too, affliction in other circumstances incur And what is that That is piggul and notar,
I will include piggul and notar, for which with karet but I will not include untithed produce, which does not of karet the verse states: “You shall afflict” “And you shall afflict your souls” include
I will include untithed produce, death but I will not include an animal carcass, is not death the verse states “you shall afflict” “and you shall afflict your souls.” includes

I will include an animal carcass, which a negative mitzva, but I will not include non-sacred, which is not a negative mitzva. the verse states “you shall afflict” “and you shall afflict your souls.” includes

And if it is your wish to say Surely, states “I will destroy that soul affliction that destroys a soul. And what is that? That is eating and drinking,

What is And if it is your wish to say? And if you say the verse is discussing those with whom relations are forbidden, the verse states: I will destroy that soul, an affliction that can cause death. And what is that? That is eating and drinking.
The school of Rabbi Yishmael taught affliction is stated here and affliction is stated further on Just as further on affliction hunger, so too, here, affliction hunger.
And let us derive it “If you shall afflict my daughters” We derive affliction the public from affliction the public, and we do not derive affliction the public from affliction an individual,

And let us derive from affliction Egypt, as it is written: “And He saw our affliction” We say that abstinence from conjugal relations. Rather, We derive affliction by the hand of God from affliction by the hand of God, and we do not derive affliction by the hand of God from affliction by the hand of man.

“Who feeds you manna in the desert in order to afflict you” Rabbi Ami and Rabbi Asi One said: There is no comparison between one who has bread in his basket and one who does not have bread in his basket. And one said: There is no comparison between one who sees and eats and one who does not see and eats

Rav Yosef said: From here allusion that blind eat but are not satisfied Abaye said: Therefore, one who has a meal should eat it only during daytime, Rabbi Zeira said: What is the verse “Better is the seeing of the eyes than the wandering of the desire” Reish Lakish said: The sight of a woman is better than the actual act as it is stated: “Better is the seeing of the eyes than the wandering of the desire.”
